reason: Adding the nodev mount option to non‑root local partitions modifies /etc/fstab and can affect how those partitions are mounted at boot. An incorrect entry may prevent the system from mounting the partition, leading to boot failure or service disruption. Because it is a filesystem/partition configuration change, it is classified as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; potential boot failure; mount misconfiguration; service disruption
